mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an open-source PostgreSQL operator designed to manage PostgreSQL databases within Kubernetes environments. It automates the entire lifecycle of PostgreSQL, from deployment to maintenance, leveraging Kubernetes' native capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and the reliance on Kubernetes for high availability without external tools.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official CloudNativePG Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://charts.cloudnative-pg.io

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for CloudNativePG.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the CloudNativePG CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for CloudNativePG to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/v1.29.0/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This installs the necessary CRDs for CloudNativePG.

steps.title: Install CloudNativePG Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the CloudNativePG operator using Helm in the 'cloudnative-pg' namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace cloudnative-pg

helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the CloudNativePG operator.
> Installs the CloudNativePG operator.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a PostgreSQL cluster using a custom resource definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

```

> This creates a PostgreSQL cluster named 'my-postgres' with 3 instances.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the PostgreSQL Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the PostgreSQL cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> This command lists all PostgreSQL clusters in the 'cloudnative-pg' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall CloudNativePG Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the CloudNativePG operator and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the PostgreSQL clusters and all associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg

```

> Uninstalls the CloudNativePG operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade CloudNativePG Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your PostgreSQL data. This step upgrades the CloudNativePG operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-cluster.yaml

helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Backup the current cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the CloudNativePG operator.
> Verify the cluster status after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-cluster.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified namespace may not exist or the CRDs are not installed.

**Fix:**
Ensure the namespace exists and CRDs are installed: `kubectl create namespace cloudnative-pg` and `kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/v1.29.0/deploy/crds.yaml`.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'PostgreSQL cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The PostgreSQL pods may not be running due to insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check resource allocation and ensure nodes meet the minimum requirements: `kubectl describe pods -n cloudnative-pg`.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The PostgreSQL service may not be properly exposed.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration: `kubectl get svc -n cloudnative-pg` and ensure the service is of type 'ClusterIP' or 'LoadBalancer'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD validation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Cluster resource definition may have incorrect specifications.

**Fix:**
Check the YAML definition for errors and ensure it matches the expected schema: `kubectl apply -f your-cluster-definition.yaml`.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for PostgreSQL 15, improved backup and restore capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the PostgreSQL cluster listed with a status of 'Ready' when running `kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg`.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
